entry.title: Deployment Readiness: SAP Datasphere & SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hello All and Happy New Year!

Beginning in February 2025, SAP Enterprise Support is offering two new pilot CQC service through the SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council (ESAC) : CQC Deployment Readiness for both SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere.
In essence, this service is intended to perform some configuration checks and ensure that customers are going live following SAP best practices.

Why: We want to ensure that customers can get started using both SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud efficiently and effectively following SAP best practices, and identify potential risks, issues, or concerns, prior to the run phase. 

What: We offer a Deployment Readiness Check service (CQC) that provides customers with a pre go-live SAP best practices review for key settings, parameters, and general configuration guidance.

 

PREREQUISITES

For participation in the SAP Datasphere workstream you would need to have the following in place:

  • SAP Datasphere Production system with connectivity to/from Datasphere
  • Models within Datasphere
  • Analytical requirements complete
  • Go-live date determined
  • SAP access to the system and required Datasphere spaces provided to perform system checks


For participation in the SAP Analytics Cloud workstream you would need to have the following in place:

  • SAP Analytics Cloud Production system provisioned  with connectivity to relevant data sources
  • Go-live ready stories developed
  • Go-live date determined
  • Technical contact person available
  • System access for SAP provided

During the pilot service delivery an SAP service engineer would connect to your system, perform the relevant checks, and provide you with a detailed report of findings from the (SAP Analytics Cloud or SAP Datasphere) system with recommendations to any resolve critical issues. 

The intention is that via delivery of this service we can enable a smooth go-live, ensure avoidance of common pitfalls, and increase the speed of adoption of your end users.

Participation

This pilot is brought to you via the SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council (ESAC).
To find out more about the SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council in general visit the website. 
Details of this workstream will soon be included on that page.

To register you interest please contact us via email: esac@sap.com
Let us know your location / timezone, rough details of your project timeline, and state your interest in this pilot workstream.

 

Further Support and Empowerment via SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps

As always, you can compliment your experience by joining the SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps.
We are providing continued support and collaboration after the EGI workshops and new pilot workshop has completed, along with further enablement materials such as best practice documents and webinars.
This is a great way for you to get access to these services, along with further support and empowerment for SAP Data and Analytics solutions.

Join the SAP Analytics Solutions value map to receive further assistance, as well as access to a dedicated learning journey on SAP Data Warehousing topics.
* S user required

 

 

 

 

 

Quicklinks

  • SAP Datasphere Fundamentals: Expert Guided Implementation
  • E-Learning: SAP Datasphere Lifecycle Management
  • E-Learning: SAP Datasphere Security & Data Access Controls
  • Practical Guide: How to build SAP Analytics Cloud stories using SAP Datasphere
  • SAP Datasphere Connectivity Series

Hi @alexander_john_berlin ,

 
1. Go in System>Overview

2.

3. Here you can find as per user and story time

If you don't have access, it won't be visible.

 

Upvote and accept it if you find it useful!

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Hi Experts, Has there been any update on transporting...
entry.content:
If you are referring to GDA webdynpro app ( rather than design studio ), the bookmark transport feature is available from certain release of embedded BW from S/4 HANA. Please refer to the following notes for details.
3438088 - How to transport Bookmarks of the Group Data Analysis Accessible (WebDynpro) app
3534793 - Transporting bookmarks from the Design Studio version of the Group Data Analysis app to the WebDynpro Version of the Group Data Analysis app

entry.title: Re: Issue with Blocked Task List Runs in BW/4 HANA Migration - Seeking Solution
entry.content: <duplicate>

entry.title: Update of SAP Learning Journey on DevOps with SAP BTP
entry.content:
Happy to announce that the learning journey Discovering DevOps with SAP BTP has received a major overhaul that went live in SAP Learning end of December 2024. Besides applying the latest branding and style changes, updates of single services and their interplay have been considered.

Screenshot of learning journey

For example, concerning pipeline flexibility of SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery service, the new integration option of SAP Cloud Transport Management into SAP Cloud ALM, or the Generative AI assistant of SAP Automation Pilot service (described in a new lesson at the end of unit 3).

Example use case for GenAI assistant of SAP Automation Pilot

Although new content has been provided, we intentionally strived to keep the learning content on beginner level. Still, also more advanced leaners should be able to benefit from the update.

If you should be interested on how to make use of agile DevOps principles for your projects on SAP BTP, this would be the opportunity to get your jump start into the offering! You can go through the whole journey or only consume those lessons you are interested in. Give it a try here, free-of-charge.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Blocked Task List Runs in BW/4 HANA Migration - Seeking Solution
entry.content: Hi Jonas, since I saw, that you posted another question during the B4H-Switch, I assumed, you found a solution for this issue here. I currently do have the same issues and it would be great, if you could share your findings.

entry.title: Re: SAP Group reporting : copying an FS Item Value to ano...
entry.content: The most straightforward way might be to leverage the Reporting Item which is technically still FS item and a shell of FS Item. It requires creating the Reporting Item hierarchy, variants etc. Or you just create reclassification rule and integrated the rule/method into data monitor / consolidation monitor. Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: Add item only for costing in production BOM
entry.content:
Hi @MySAPuser2002 ,

To assign a cost to a text item in the BOM:

Add Text Item to BOM: Use item category T (Text) in CS02 and enter a description (e.g., "In-house Liquid").

Assign Cost: In the BOM Costing tab, input a manual price for the text item.

Costing Variant Settings: Ensure the costing variant includes text items.

Run Cost Estimate: Use CK11N or check the process order cost analysis to confirm the liquid's value is included.

After creating the process order, check the cost analysis in the order via CO03 or KKS1 to confirm the liquid's cost is included.

Kindly upvote and accept if you found this helpful!

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Copy adobe form -MM_PUR_RFQ_INTERNAL fragment based
entry.content:
We succeed to have a view on the form but it is not possible to attached the XDP and SDP to this forum.

We have copied the form, directly from the Fiori tile, to a custom one and have been able to open the custom one.

entry.title: Re: Inventory Accounting with Universal Parallel Accounting in SAP S/4HANA 2022
entry.content:
@WeekendVampire You cannot mix the classic approach with the settings in the currency and valuation profile and the new approach using universal parallel accounting in the same system. The Brazil country version hasn't been adjusted to be compatible with universal parallel accounting, so at the moment, if you are operating in Brazil, you will have to stay with the classic approach. 

Regards, Janet

entry.title: Re: Copy adobe form -MM_PUR_RFQ_INTERNAL fragment based
entry.content: But it doesn't solve the Data source issue where the one assigned to the MM_PUR_RFQ_INTERNAL form doesn't exist.

entry.title: Elevate Your SAP Commerce Cloud Certification Journey with Our Certification Live Sessions
entry.content:
Abstract

In the ever-evolving world of SAP Commerce Cloud, achieving success means staying up-to-date with the latest features and insights. In response to the updated certification exams (2411) for business and technical users at the end of 2024, we are excited to offer two comprehensive certification preparation live sessions in Q1 2025. These sessions are designed to equip you with the tools and knowledge needed to excel in the crucial SAP Commerce Cloud certification exams.

Please be aware that any certification live session recordings you might encounter from before 2025 are outdated in relation to the current certification exams.

Live Sessions Overview

Live Session 1 (1 hour): Certification Prep for SAP Commerce Cloud – Business User

Purpose: This session is tailored to support participants preparing for the SAP Certified Application Associate - SAP Commerce Cloud Business User exam.

Certification Level: Associate

Target Audience: Business Consultants, Technology Consultants, Software Architects, and anyone interested in taking the certification exam.

Agenda:

  • Essential Topics Focus: Concentrate on key subjects from the Business User Certification Exam, emphasizing critical components that will be evaluated.
  • Strategic Preparation Guidance: Receive expert advice on crafting an effective study plan for associate-level success.

Live Session 2 (1 hour): Certification Prep for SAP Commerce Cloud - Developer

Purpose: This session is dedicated to empowering participants for the SAP Certified Development Professional - SAP Commerce Cloud Developer exam.

Certification Level: Professional

Target Audience: Software Developers, Software Architects, Technology Consultants, and anyone interested in taking the certification exam.

Agenda:

  • Key Topics Exploration: Dive into the main topics of the Developer Certification Exam, highlighting crucial elements that will be assessed.
  • Expert Preparation Tips: Gain insights into creating a robust study plan for professional-level certification success.

Common Elements Across Both Sessions

  • Comprehensive Coverage: Each session will systematically explore the exam topics, ensuring thorough understanding of essential components.
  • Interactive Learning: Engage with the content through interactive sessions, enriched by instructors' expertise and collective group insights.

Preferred Written Learning Materials?

If you prefer written guides, explore our study guides for both Business User and Developer exams to complement your preparation:

  • Study Guide for SAP Certified Associate - Business User - SAP Commerce Cloud Certification Exam 2411
  • Study Guide for SAP Certified Professional - Developer - SAP Commerce Cloud Certification Exam 2411

Already Certified?

If you've already achieved SAP Commerce Cloud certification in 2024 or before, remember to renew it by retaking the updated exam or participating in the stay certified programs. Look for email notifications from SAP about these programs and consider the following courses as preparation:

  1. Stay Certified – SAP Commerce Cloud Business User
  2. Stay Certified – SAP Commerce Cloud Developer

Conclusion

These upcoming live sessions in Q1 2025 are designed to be pivotal in your SAP Commerce Cloud certification journey. Whether you're a budding developer or a seasoned business user, expect in-depth coverage of essential exam topics and strategic preparation advice. This is about more than just passing exams - it's about becoming a leader in the SAP Commerce Cloud ecosystem.

Thanks for reading, and we look forward to seeing you at the sessions!

entry.title: Re: Story usage by user
entry.content: Hello and thank you again for your reply. What I am looking for is the information if particular users did not open a particular story in a certain time frame not how often the story was opened in general. I think I worded it poorly initially. Thank you anyways!

entry.title: AI Features to Enhance PMO Performance
entry.content:
How AI Features Can Improve PMO Performance

The Program Management Office (PMO) is a group or department that oversees the external workforce management program, handling and managing daily operations in the SAP Fieldglass application. The PMO is important for maintaining program efficiency and smooth technology operation.

AI Features That Can Help PMO Efficiency

SAP Fieldglass' AI enhances procurement, supporting PMOs in proactive staffing, risk mitigation, competitive pricing, and efficient workflow management. It helps to improve efficiency and decision-making in managing contingent and services workflows. Are you part of the PMO group in SAP Fieldglass? Let’s explore the available AI features and see how they can assist you.

AI-Assisted Job Description Creation

SAP Fieldglass offers an AI job description tool that creates and enhances job postings using generative AI. This tool lets you input job title, description, and required skills for relevant results.

See the Required Setup to use AI for job posting descriptions.

AI-Assisted Job Description Translation

SAP Fieldglass auto-translates job postings using AI, reducing manual effort, standardizing processes, and supporting global expansion.

See the Required Setup to use AI for job posting description translation.

Usage of AI to generate and translate job posting descriptions:         

AI-Assisted SOW Description Creation

The AI-Assisted SOW Description feature helps you to create a statement of work description based on various elements of the SOW.

AI in SOW creation reduces the back-and-forth communication with suppliers. Simple data input is needed to generate comprehensive SOW descriptions, enhancing clarity for suppliers and accelerating collaboration.

See the Required Setup to use AI for generating SOW description creation.

Usage of AI to generate SOW description:             

Using Machine Learning Features for Candidate Analysis

Machine Learning helps hiring managers and PMOs find the best candidates by evaluating their experience, skills, and resumes. It helps keeping the hiring decisions fair and impartial.

Key features include:

Resume Assessment

Resume assessment scores with job descriptions, removing personal information for fair and unbiased results.

See the Required Setup to use the Resume Assessment feature.

Experience Assessment

The Experience Assessment evaluates candidates’ fit with job requirements, providing Related Experience (percentage that indicates how well their experience matches the job requirements) and Industry Match (keyword comparison that indicates if there's a strong match).

See the Required Setup to use the Experience Assessment feature.

Highlighting Skills

The Highlighting Skills auto-detects and highlights key skills on resumes, saving time and providing quick assessment of candidate strengths and fit.

See the Required Setup to use the Highlighting Skills feature.

 

ℹ️  The Generative AI features are available for you if you have the RISE Premium Edition or the RISE Premium Plus Edition package.

See Also 

Company Configuration: AI and Machine Learning Features

entry.title: Career shift from ABAP to AL/ML
entry.content:
Dear All,

I have 18 years of experience in SAP ABAP(ECC). I am keen on learning AI/ML from career point of view.

If I learn AI/ML,

1)Would my ABAP experience be gone or its better to move into SAP-BTP?

2)I will mostly continue with SAP Partners company (TCS/DELOITTE/Capgemini etc.), so will I end up as end user of SAP developed AI/ML Tools like Joule or would there be enhancements in standard AL/ML(Like we have enhancements in RICEFW over standards) as well in these SAP Partners company (TCS/DELOITTE/Capgemini etc.)? Can you please name some of the areas/Modules requiring the enhancement on AI/ML side?

3) Do I still need to learn SAP BTP in case I learn AI/ML?

4) Responses of AI/ML doesn't seem 100% accurate and trustworthy. So would AI/ML going to be bubble.

Regards,

Sanjay

thanks

We currently have the same need, I wanted to know if since you opened the SR until today you have been able to manage it, that is, have you been able to make the SWIFT field mandatory for the supplier? And if so, could you give us advice on how to activate it?

Thanks you. 

Diana

entry.title: Customer Onboarding for SAP Field Service Management
entry.content:
Are you a new SAP Field Service Management Customer ready to start your project implementation?

To ensure a successful start to your SAP project, we’ve designed a Customer Onboarding Journey dedicated to SAP Field Service Management.

Start your Onboarding Journey today by:

  1. Visiting the SAP Field Service Management Onboarding Resource Center, your one-stop shop for a smooth onboarding process. Access ungated enablement resources, including guides on how to use SAP for Me, how to access your system, and useful information on what to expect and how to prepare for the entire project implementation.

  2. Registering for the Onboarding Webcasts, which are quarterly live sessions designed to get you up to speed with the onboarding process. Plan, register, and join these sessions to gain insights and clarify any questions you may have. If you can't join live, on-demand content is also available.

The 4 phases of your Onboarding Journey:

The content from the SAP Field Service Management Onboarding Resource Center and Onboarding Webcasts is organized into 4 phases:

 

This is the content in more detail:

Phase 1. Get Started with SAP 

The main objective is to make sure customers understand the SAP Field Service Management system provisioning and access process and become familiar with the expected communications from SAP.

Additionally, we aim to make sure customers are aware of SAP channels, particularly SAP for Me, and know how to get support from SAP for both technical and non-technical issues.

Main topics covered:

  1. Get to know your onboarding journey
  2. Learn about SAP channels and s-user setup
  3. Get access to your system
  4. Get familiar with SAP Support

Phase 2. Plan your Implementation

The main objective is to provide the customer a high-level knowledge on how to better run, manage and accelerate their SAP Field Service Management project implementation using the tools and resources included in their subscription.

Main topics covered:

  1. Run your project implementation with SAP Activate
  2. Manage your project with SAP Cloud ALM
  3. Learn about the offerings and channels that can support your implementation
  4. Accelerate your project with SAP Best Practices
  5. Learn about roles & system testing

Phase 3. Get Enabled

The main objective is to present the customer with free learning and enablement resources to help them effectively use the SAP Field Service Management solution.

Main topics covered:

  1. Get to know the key features of SAP Field Service Management
  2. Learn about the offerings and channels that can support you with having a deeper knowledge of your solution 
  3. Check how you can interact with SAP Experts and Peers

Phase 4. Prepare to Go Live

The main objective is to ensure a smooth go-live and prepare customers to run their business effectively.

Main topics covered:

  1. Get ready for final project tasks
  2. Learn about the offerings and channels that can support you after your go-live
  3. Keep innovating after go-live

In Conclusion:

The SAP Field Service Management Onboarding Journey is meticulously designed to ensure that you’re fully prepared for a successful implementation, since contract signature to go-live.

Take the time to explore, plan, and engage with the resources at your disposal. The road to a successful go-live with SAP Field Service Management starts here.

entry.title: Re: visual code studio command run failed with error : sp...
entry.content: Thanks ! Indeed, setting : "@sap/ux-ui5-tooling": "1.13.3"

entry.title: Re: BAPI to create handling units in SAP EWM?
entry.content: Thanks Dominik. That's what I was suspecting. It's interesting that the custom program uses BAPI_HU_CREATE and that works as long as HU's being passes fall within range shown in ERP HU intervals.

entry.title: TVRF no longer to be uploaded from PCA, but CAA. What remains for PCA?
entry.content:

when you access the indirect sales support page you will see the attached info. What does this exactly mean? Does the PCA still do the TA and fill out all questions in the TVRF? Where is the cut to the CAA? Is there any more detailed info on this? This came out of nowhere for me.

Greets

Sebastian

entry.title: Logon to system SAP failed (partner '122.176.112.165:3326' not reached) error
entry.content:
Hi Expets,

I'm trying to connect to the SAP system from Eclipse, but I'm facing the error 'Logon to system SAP failed (partner '122.176.112.165:3326' not reached)'.

Note: I can log in to the SAP GUI system using my username and password.

The problem is when I'm trying to connect Eclipse to my SAP system

I'm using a demo Hana system.

Your support is highly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Installing Composable Storefront for SAP Commerce 2211.20
entry.content: Hi @Rakesh_kp . It appears you're talking to the wrong server, and you need to take a look at the .npmrc file in the current directory. That's where the npm command (also the ng command) looks to know what repository to query. You need to query the SAP Repository-Based Shipment Channel (RBSC), not the public open source repository. That file also needs to contain valid NPM Base64 Credentials (which you generated as part of these instructions). Also, check that your user has a license for Spartacus.

entry.title: Why do we need SAP Asset Performance Management (SAP APM)?
entry.content:
Everything evolves, and maintenance is no exception- continuously evolving into a more streamlined and efficient process!

With evolving technology, the maintenance practices have improved as well. With the advent of Industry 4.0 and availability of IoT there are new avenues for maintenance as well, which are cost effective and increases the reliability of machines. Data science, AI and ML can be used to predict and avoid failures based on historical data.

Maintenance always has budget constraints and I’m yet to see an industry where maintenance has unlimited resources to spend and keep the plant running without issues. Improved maintenance processes can save a lot of money and resources, while also increasing the reliability and predictability of the machines.

Historical Maintenance Process:

Historically, the only maintenance that we used to do was Reactive maintenance, which means you maintain something only when it breaks. This could be either corrective or breakdown in nature. The problem with this kind of maintenance is it is very costly and can cause long breakdowns where a line, a section or the whole plan is inoperative.

The reason Reactive maintenance is expensive has 3 major factors:

  1. Possible loss of production.
  2. Unplanned human resource requirements.
  3. Unplanned spare part requirements.

 

Current Maintenance Processes:

Apart from the historical Reactive maintenance, the maintenance practices slowly evolved into Proactive maintenance, which means doing repetitive maintenance tasks for instance changing certain spare parts of a machine such as gaskets, o-rings or changing the lubricating oil on a regular basis. The schedule or interval could be suggested by the OEMs or could be based on the experience of the operator. This was effective and helped avoid breakdowns. However, this was still expensive as the parts were being changed even when the parts are in good condition. This also might have environmental impact due to wastage of parts and oil. Proactive maintenance is still the most prevalent maintenance strategy in most industries.

The reason Proactive maintenance is expensive has 2 major factors:

  1. Excessive spare part consumption.
  2. Increased human resource requirements.

Benefits of Proactive maintenance:

  1. Increased reliability.
  2. Reduced unplanned shutdowns.

 

Future Maintenance Processes using SAP APM:

  • ­The next evolution in maintenance is Predictive and Prescriptive maintenance.

Predictive maintenance is a way to predict the future failures so that timely actions could be taken to plan and handle the failure. Prescriptive maintenance though on top of predicting the failure, also prescribes actions that can be taken to avoid that future failure.

For example, if the RPM of a purifier is lower than normal, then using SAP APM’s Asset Health module an Alert or a Notification could be generated to inspect the purifier.

  • Another evolution in maintenance is RCM- Reliability Centered Maintenance.

RCM dictates identifying critical assets/ machines, defining failure modes based on the operating context and creating a strategy for the maintenance of identified machines.

For example, we identify a fuel oil purifier as a critical piece of equipment since it supplies fuel oil to the main engine. We define operating context as heavy oil as the medium the purifier is processing. Then we define the maintenance strategies on it based on the experience and history of purifiers with the same operating context (fuel oil). Maintenance strategies can be defined in the form of reactive, proactive or improvement recommendations. The recommendations could be converted into time or counter based (for ex. run hours) maintenance plans and task lists. These strategies could be revisited based on the feedback and thus can be improved continuously.

This could also aid in predictive maintenance. For instance, while working on the notification created in the example under predictive/ prescriptive maintenance and converting it into a Work Order a task list could be included based on the Recommendations from SAP APM’s Asset Reliability Engineering module.

Benefits of Predictive maintenance:

  1. Increased reliability.
  2. Reduced maintenance cost.
  3. Consistently improving maintenance program.

 

So, to answer the original question which a lot of prospective customers’ would be asking, “why do we need SAP Asset Performance Management (APM)” -  SAP APM with it’s Asset Health and Asset Reliability Engineering modules can help industries transition from the traditional reactive & proactive maintenance to predictive, prescriptive maintenance and RCM. This will help in overall improvement of maintenance processes, increase in reliability and reduction in maintenance costs.

You can also read my other blogs:

What is SAP Asset Performance Management?

SAP APM in Maritime Industry

entry.title: Re: Call SAP Digital Manufacturing Production Processes via REST API

KR,
Sunny

entry.title: Re: Printouts of Accounting documents (Portugal)
entry.content:
Please follow the explanation under  

Outgoing Invoices with Digital Signatures in Financial Accounting

Printing Outgoing Invoices with Digital Signatures | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Exploring the RSS feeds of the new SAP Community platform
entry.content: @mamartins @rkaouass I was facing the same issue when trying to refresh the feed when using Miniflux. I was able to work around it by specifying a custom user agent in the feed configuration, e.g. Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:93.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/93.0. I'm not sure this is possible in the readers that you are using.

entry.title: Re: Default Notification Processing Context in Fiori App 'Create Maintenance Request' (F5111A)

Have you found any solution for this, we have the same requirement and need to default the Notification processing context in the Create Maintenance Request app.

entry.title: Re: Is SQL Anywhere viable and sustainable
entry.content: FWIW, the linked "SAP SQL Anywhere Road Map" pdf has the PDF title "SAP Product Road Map 2018" and was created in 2021/02, so there's that...

entry.title: Re: Default Notification Processing Context in Fiori App...
entry.content: You can use BAdI EAM_NOTIFICATION_FIELD_CONTROL to define mandatory fields; default values can be maintained in technical catalog.

entry.title: Re: SAP Tech Bytes: Using the SAP Approuter at dev time - a full-stack journey with UI5 and SAP CAP
entry.content:
Hi @ahmetdik1,

Did you do a proper cds bind in the CAP server root before running the dev-approuter?

Thanks for the answer. I have already tried to default the value in my Business Catalog for the Notification Processing Context and it still does not work. Am I missing something here.

entry.title: Re: FI-GL account balance and open Line items (template) Error
entry.content:
Hi Chempian,

Could it be that there's a reconciliation account involved? See e.g. here: https://me.sap.com/notes/3091350/E

Best regards,

Verena

entry.title: Can I overlap production orders? (Not operations)
entry.content:
Good Afternoon,

Is there a way, from a capacity planning perspective, to overlap production orders?

At the moment this is what appears to happen:

Part A on Prod Ord 1 for qty 10, takes 5 days to make, and is required to make Part B.

Part B on Prod Ord 2 for qty 10 takes 10 days to make.

SAP will say the process to make 10x Part B (including the Part A sub assembly) will take 15 days

What I want to see is SAP telling me that instead of 15 days, this would take 10.5 days, because after the first half day I would have completed 1 Part A, which then allows be to start building part B.

Is this at all possible??

entry.title: Re: API REST integration (new UI)
entry.content:
Hello there, 

Just wanted to jump also to this topic as the new UI was causing me some headache as well when it came out. I didn't bother too much with it, rather just duplicated some old REST API resources, and could keep on with those. 

But I did find some pretty good use of it in finally being able to implement `PATCH` requests to a Xano backend. 

Some useful thoughts could potentially be: 
1. for simple query parameter setting you can use the `APPEND_URL_PARAMETERS()` formula to easily combine an object or variable into a GET query string. 

2. to ensure that your backend (in our case Xano) can identify the content of a POST/PUT/PATCH request, we found it useful to also wrap the request body into an `ENCODE_JSON()` formula. 

3. this is something I plan on exploring a little bit more: it seems that this new UI also enables the REST api to be used with Data Components (even including pagination)

I honestly hope that the UI can get slightly better and there can be more documentation about this kind of implementation, but until then this page helped me a lot: 
SAP BTP REST direct integration as it seems that the new universal REST API integration is just an SAP BTP REST integration, without predefined routes. Also I presume that as we can slowly notice other significant UI changes, I presume that the REST api integration also goes into this direction: SAP Build Help centre

entry.title: Raising the production order quantity and releasing with ppd
entry.content:
I am receiving a quantity confirmation through a PLC trigger, there will be occasions where we receive a confirmation that is higher than the SFC quantity in work at that operation. Example we have receive a confirmation of 20 when only 10 sfc quantity is left to complete the full SFC quantity.

 

Is there a way to raise the build quantity and release the new quantity released, so I can post the confirmation? I have done this with SAP MII but can't find a function block/BAPI to achieve this with SAP DM.
Thanks

entry.title: Utilization In Hours in Manage Work Center Capacity
entry.content:
hi

I don't know the formula for Utilization In Hours…

 

 

this shifts

 

this Standard value 

 

this step up Formula

this Processing Formula

 

How do I do the calculation to get 32.55H?

Is there any information missing?

entry.title: Re: How to update the Passord Policy of IAS users via RES...
entry.content: Thanks Marko. So I could force users to change their password, is there any way to do that via REST api? I mean to mark the user with some flag?

entry.title: Re: The Required Functional modules Knowledge.
entry.content:
Hi @Sadory 

In general, there are four Modules that have repeated during my developer career: Material Management, Procurement, Sales and Distribution and Finance.

For MM: I suggest to have an overall understanding of Materials, Also understand the terms Plants and Storage Locations.

For Procurement: Have an overall understanding of Purchase Orders, Inbound Deliveries and Service Entry Sheets.

For SD: Have an overall understanding of Sales Orders, Outbound Deliveries and Invoices. Also understand the terms Sales Organization and Distribution Channel.

For FI: Have an overall understanding of Billing Documents, Payments and Validations/Substitutions. Also understand the terms Company Code, Account, Cost Center.

Of course, every module is made of tons of other terms, but in my case, understanding the terms I mentioned above helped me to better communicate with Functional Consultants.

entry.title: Re: Why does the Database User from a SPACE in SAP Datasp...
entry.content:
In SAP HANA database there is a view SYS.AUTHENTICATION_ERROR_DETAILS: https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud-database/sap-hana-cloud-sap-hana-database-security-guide/troubleshooting-authentication-problems

But I am not sure if it is accessible from SAP Datasphere.

entry.title: Re: How to Validate the Data for Field in CDS View
entry.content: If I understand you correctly, you want to know which underlying tables and fields your data source is based on. You can open the CDS view in ADT (Eclipse) and drill down to the tables that are used.

entry.title: Re: MDG DQM Validation Rules – Access additional data model tables without ABAP coding
entry.content:
Hello,

it is important to note that the change request and the process differ in the fields that are filled.

The components Process ID, Step Number and Source System are only filled if the processing takes place within a process. This is the case with the Evaluate Data Quality process.

The Source ID is filled in both the change request and the process. As can be seen in your screenshots, it is filled in both cases. With the change request, the primary key of the object currently being processed is filled here. For the BP, this is the BP number.

Best regards
Stefan Weingand

entry.title: Re: Landed Costs with Fixed Assets shows error
entry.content: Hi Felipe_Lima, Yes all Landed Costs types have a G/L account assigned and also I added screenshot in the english language to question

entry.title: Re: Anticipo Proveedor con OC de Activo Fijo
entry.content:
Hi SFerrand,

Unfortunately,  it is impossible to post a downpayment with a purchase order associated with a Fixed asset in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

You can create an Investment Project (WBS) and then the purchase order with the AccClassCategory Projects.  

In the purchase order, you can add the values of the downpayment

And create the downpayment request with the Monitor Purchase Order Down Payments app

You must run the whole purchase order cycle and settle the project to the final fixed asset.

SAP Note 3154459 3154459 - AA834 / F5378 / SFIN_FI017 when posting a downpayment to an asset SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Best regards

 

Edna

entry.title: Re: In SAC Data Wrangler, can I generate multiple rows of...
entry.content:
Thanks for your response.

Can you elaborate a little more on what you are suggesting. Would I need to land the base data first into a staging model and then use a Data Action with the Advanced Formula to convert that single row into 6 rows of data and post it into the final model?

entry.title: Re: UKM_BP error R1 206 when BP Role is not the standard...
entry.content:
Read 3502521 - Error in transaction UKM_BP: "BP role UKM000 does not exist" (message number: R1206) 

Basically this role is hard-coded in the transaction 😒 actually you are calling BP with some hidden parameter id in memory.

 

REPORT  ukm_bp_start.

PARAMETERS p_id TYPE bu_partner NO-DISPLAY.

DATA:
  lc_role    LIKE bus_joel_main-partner_role VALUE 'UKM000',
  tcod       LIKE sy-tcode VALUE 'BP',
  lv_partner TYPE bu_partner.

 

Did you try to create a Z-transaction changing the default value for role in the wrapper report ?

entry.title: SAP-CRM V7.13 (non-HANA): Divide data into 2 clients to support a carve out
entry.content: Currently one client (100) consists of data of 2 divisions. That should change: Every division should become an own client (Client 100 and 200 on the same system). I have the idea to simply create a second Client 200 and go through all datatables (Masterdata and transactions), starting with BUT000 and COMM_PRODUCT and simply change the Clientnumber to 200 for the data of the carved out division via SQL? It appears to be just a minor effort to cascade down from BUT000 and COMM_PRODUCT to related masterdatatables and transactiontables. The result would be that within our database-tables we would have data of the 2 clients. Are there any concerns of doing that? To me it appears the simplest way, just simple updates via DBACockpit, no deletes. I have the impression that consulting companies and also SAP are not recommending that and instead prefer downloads and uploads, sometime via Excel, which leads to serious issues getting the correct state of transactions and moving history data. I also see some challenges related to the datamodel, like CRMD_LINK... and the integration to ERP, but that is far less critical to me than any other approach. Does anyone have experience with that approach?

entry.title: Alternate resources (via WC classification)not creating multiple modes in PDS
entry.content:
Dear Experts, 

We are trying to create the possibility to re-schedule production orders on alternate resources in ePPDS via WC classification. Following are the steps done but the PDS do not have multiple modes for each alternate WC. 

1. WCs are classified via a 019 classification 

2. All WCs are CIF'ed 

3. BoM, Routing (Standard WC), PV created and CIF'ed 

4. Only one mode is visible in the PDS. 

We looked at the validity dates of the characteristic, classification, routings etc. and still cannot identify the root-cause. 

 

Looking forward to read your opinions and advice on how to set this up in the system.

 

Thanks,

Sandeep

entry.title: Tenant and domain in SAP Analytic Cloud
entry.content:
Hello everyone, I have a question about SAP Analytics Cloud versions. For one client, I have the information summarized in the attached screenshot "ClientWithDifferentTenant.png," where the SAC tenant has the following domain: "https://******q.eu10.analytics.cloud.sap" Meanwhile, for most other clients, I have the configuration shown in the screenshot "ClientHCSTenant.png," and the tenant has the following domain: "https://******.eu10.hcs.cloud.sap"

Could you please explain the difference between these two SAC versions? Are there any limitations associated with the "analytics.cloud" tenant?

Thanks

Dino

entry.title: Re: Filtering table measures with a dropdown list
entry.content:
Hi @Rick05,

I tried the same scenario filter measures in table I have below measure's as shown in table 

2. I used below script on dropdown.

var selectedMeasure = Dropdown_3.getSelectedKey();
if(selectedMeasure ==="Quantity"){
Table_5.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter(Alias.MeasureDimension,["Quantity"]);

}else{

Table_5.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter(Alias.MeasureDimension,["Discount"])
;
Table_5.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter(Alias.MeasureDimension,["Profit"]);
Table_5.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter(Alias.MeasureDimension,["Sale"]);

3. Its filter according to measures selection

4. If I select Sale it will filter as per Sale's Measure 

OR

Same Scenario you can do with "Measure Input Control" you don't need to be write script it will work on both chart and table

2. Add Measure Input control and remove other measure's

 

Kindly upvote if you find it helpful!

 

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Identity Services for Beginners
entry.content: thanks PriyankaChak . I really appreciate your efforts. I recently moved from other cloud storage. this blog really helped. Continue share your experience in this amazing community

entry.title: Re: Logon to system SAP failed (partner '122.176.112.165:3326' not reached) error
entry.content: It depends on your system, please contact your system administrator.

entry.title: Re: Transport from ECC to S/4 Hana
entry.content: Create a transport request of type transport of copy, add your program in this transport request, and transport it. Ask your administrator to do the transport.

entry.title: Re: Transport from ECC to S/4 Hana
entry.content: Read 1090842 - Composite SAP Note: Cross-release transports and look for tricks such as Move from One System to another without transport Route. Did you have so many code to copy that transport would be a solution?

entry.title: Re: Raising the production order quantity and releasing w...
entry.content:
Hi, see below some options you can evaluate:

- Increasing the Build Quantity for Overproduction manually in Manage Orders app and release more SFCs/quantities if Tolerances % are set from ERP, which is a manual option and might not be a good fit to your use case.

- SFC Split, where Allow Overproduction flag is available, so via Production Process you should be able to split the existing SFC into new child SFCs with extra quantity for every new overproduction confirmation that comes in. Example:

- Set SFC Quantity, where you can change quantity at SFC level directly. As a remark, only New and In Queue SFCs can have the quantity updated, so if the SFC is In Work in your case, you would need to SignOff the SFC first before setting the SFC quantity via API and then start it again via Production Process. Example:

Hope it helps! Br,
Manoel

entry.title: Re: Could not create instance under may space under Subac...
entry.content:
You created your HANA Cloud for "Other Environments". Normally, you would be able to add an instance mapping to Cloud Foundry but that doesn't work on BTP Trial.

To create HDI containers in your Cloud Foundry space, you'll need to delete your HANA Cloud instance and recreate it in the Cloud Foundry environment. Then, go to your trial subaccount and create a subscription to SAP HANA Cloud and choose the plan "tools" (see screenshot).

 Once the subscription has been created, go to Security -> Role Rollections and assign yourself the roles SAP HANA Cloud Administrator and SAP HANA Cloud Security Administrator.

Then go to the SAP HANA Cloud Central (the tools plan that you've just subscribed to). There, you can create a HANA Cloud instance and choose Cloud Foundry as Runtime Environment:

 Select your space, create it and all wil be as expected.

I have query program name GP75NENJF2H5YMJA3Z7IAU99W4S .

How to find the query name for this query report or program.

entry.title: Re: Code List based on CDS View doesn't show View Entitie...
entry.content: Thank you Karsten! I have followed all the steps reported on help.sap.com. I have created the CDS custom entity and the service too. Checking the service I can see the data, but I still cannot find the CDS View into the list, so I cannot use it for my custom field. I cannot undersand why is so complex

entry.title: Re: Generating firmed Planned orders
entry.content:
Good day.

Sounds like you have the firming time fence set on these particular items. This means someone has set the planning time fence and planning cycle (MRP1). It is working with the planning calendar (MRP2) and all other MRP trigger settings placed in the MRP tabs of the material master. If you set the planning time fence and the planning cycle, MRP will automatically firm all planned procurement orders during that time frame indicated. Remove the planning time fence entry and the planning cycle entry and orders will no longer come through in firmed status.

Ganesh N

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite: Education on How to Deploy it for HR (January 22nd Webinar)
entry.content:
To help with understanding how SAP Integration Suite can improve the efficiency and effectiveness of integrations between HR systems (such as with SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central core HR application), one of our partners (Comentec) is hosting an executive briefing specifically designed for HR and HR IT leaders.

  • Topic: "Maximizing Your HR Cloud Journey: Strategic Integration for Employee Central"
  • Date: January 22, 2025
  • Time: 12:00 PM EST / 18:00 CET

This session is particularly valuable for customers who:

  1. Are maintaining legacy integrations alongside Employee Central (on-premise, third-party, and/or using bespoke integration tools)
  2. Want to improve their integration operational excellence and/or modernization timeline

Topics to be covered include:

  • HR customer success stories of using SAP Integration Suite with Employee Central
  • Frameworks for assessing your current needs and facilitating discussions about integration
  • Integration ROI examples
  • Debunking common misconceptions about SAP Integration Suite

Registration Link: https://us06web.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_-zjq5jhRSwmRvKkDN68SjA

entry.title: Rebates (Condition Contracts) Partial Settlements with individual business volume
entry.content:
Partial Settlement refers to the process of making a partial payment or credit settlement against a rebate agreement. This typically occurs when a customer or business partner earns a rebate, but the full amount due under the agreement isn't paid in one go. Instead, a portion of the rebate is settled, leaving the remainder for future settlement. As an example, an annual rebate agreement with partial settlement every quarter.

Let us under how the partial settlement of rebate condition contract works in S4 with an example:

There is an agreement with the customer stipulating that they will receive a 5% rebate on their business volume, provided they make purchases totaling $100,000.

 

Quarterly Sales

Rebate amount

Q1

$50,000

0

Q2

$75,000

$6,250

Q3

$100,000

$5,000

Q4

$75,000

$3,750

 

Let’s walk through how the rebate amount is calculated each quarter. In Q1, the total sales amount is $50,000, which does not meet the required business volume of $100,000. Therefore, the rebate for Q1 is zero. By the end of Q2, the cumulative business volume reaches $125,000 (Q1: $50,000 + Q2: $75,000). The rebate is calculated on the total of $125,000, resulting in a rebate of $6,250. By the end of Q3, the total business volume increases to $225,000. A 5% rebate on this amount equals $11,250. However, since $6,250 has already been settled in Q2, this amount is deducted from the $11,250, leaving a Q3 settlement amount of $5,000.

This process follows the standard functionality in the CCM rebate agreements.

Individual Partial Settlement:

Let's examine how individual partial settlements operate. Business volumes are not cumulative; instead, each quarter's business volume is assessed independently. According to the annual agreement, the customer must purchase products worth $100,000 within a quarter to qualify for the rebate for that specific quarter

 

Quarterly Sales

Rebate amount

Q1

$50,000

0

Q2

$75,000

0

Q3

$100,000

$5,000

Q4

$75,000

0

 

Configuration required:

Menu path: WLFA -> Condition Contract Management -> Condition Contract Settlement -> Specify Settlement Settings for Condition Contract Types

In the “Settlement Dates” section, for Partial settlement type select 2-Separate.

entry.title: SAP EWM Intercompany STO Process Between 2 EWM Managed Warehouses Using SPED
entry.content:
Introduction:

The Document will discuss the STO process between 2 EWM-based warehouses belonging to 2 different company codes. The process talks about the steps and configurations that are required to successfully run the process in the S/4 Managed EWM system. Since there are various documents available where movement types 643 and 101 are used to do this process hence we will be dealing with valuated stock i.e. the goods issue will be done via movement type 683 and the goods receipt will be done via movement type 107 and 109. We will also be automating the process of Inbound delivery via SPED output type.

One of the most important points to note here is that as we are using cross-delivery HUs hence the HU’s will be transferred from the outbound delivery to the inbound delivery if the receiving warehouse is also managed by an EWM system. 

However, this process does not work if you have activated a unique number assignment for HUs. As a best practice whenever you have multiple EWM systems in your landscape as a good practice the HU number range should be different in both warehouses.

Please read more FAQ about this in SAP note 1119073 

 

STO FLOW:

 

  1. A Purchase Order is created to move stock between two EWM-managed warehouses.
  2. Outbound delivery is generated via VL10B.
  3. Outbound delivery is distributed to EWM Automatically.
  4. Picking is done via /SCWM/PRDO or RF Device.
  5. Goods issue is reflected back in outbound delivery in S/4 landscape by creating Material document with movement type 683 and 107.
  6. SPED output type is created and processed to create inbound delivery for receiving of stock.
  7. HU which were generated during repacking are attached to inbound delivery.
  8. Inbound Delivery is gets distributed in the EWM system.
  9. Goods receipt and putaway is done in the EWM system via /SCWM/PRDI or RF Device.
  10. Goods receipt is reflected in S/4 Landscape by creating a Material document with movement type 109.

 

 


Pre-Requisites for Intercompany STO Process:

  • The HU Number range should be unique in both the warehouses.
  • The HU Number range of the 1st warehouse should be maintained as the external number range in the 2nd warehouse.
  • The HU Number range of the 2nd warehouse should be maintained as the external number range in the 1st warehouse.

 

Configuration Required:

Define Shipping Data for Plants:

SPRO –> IMG –> Material Management –> Purchasing –> Purchase order –> Set up Stock Transport order –> Define Shipping Data for Plants

 

 

Configure Delivery Type & Availability Check Procedure by Plant:

SPRO –> IMG –> Material Management –> Purchasing –> Purchase order –> Set up Stock Transport order –> Assign Delivery Type and Checking Rule

 

Assign Document Type, One-Step Procedure, under delivery Tolerance:

SPRO –> IMG –> Material Management –> Purchasing –> Purchase order –> Set up Stock Transport order –> Assign Document Type, One-Step Procedure, Under delivery Tolerance

 

Define Confirmation Control:

Check the confirmation control key 0004 (Inbound Delivery) in Customizing for Materials Management, under Purchasing  Confirmations  Set Up Confirmation Control.

Under Confirmation Sequence, the two checkboxes GR-Relevant and GR with Confirmation must be set.

Set Up Condition Type SPED:

Check output determination procedure V10000 (Header Output) in Customizing for Logistics Execution, under Shipping  Basic Shipping Functions  Output Control  Output Determination  Maintain Output Determination for Outbound Deliveries  Maintain Output Determination Procedure.

Under Control Data, there must be an entry with condition type SPED (Stock Transfer Generates Inbound Delivery) and condition 408 (Stock Transfer Create ID).

Define Delivery type:

Check confirmation category LA (Inbound Delivery) in Customizing for Materials Management, under Purchasing  Confirmations  Define Internal Confirmation Categories.

The Delivery Type Inbound Delivery Stock in Transit field must contain an inbound delivery type for the process with stock in transit. We will be using the standard delivery type ELST (Inbound Delivery Receiving Valuated Stock in Transit). 

This delivery type must use an item category that uses movement type 109 (Goods Receipt from Valuated Goods Receipt Blocked Stock) or another movement type with posting string reference 109.

 

Define Item Categories for Delivery (Inbound):

In Customizing for Logistics Execution, under Shipping in the Customizing for Deliveries use the activity Define Item Categories to Deliveries, ensure that movement type 109 or a movement type with posting string reference 109 is assigned to this item category.

 

 

In Customizing for Logistics Execution, in the Customizing activity Define Item Category Determination in Deliveries, and check the item category that is assigned to this delivery type.

 

 

 

Define Item Categories for Delivery (Outbound):

As per above mentioned configuration we are using delivery type NCCR for triggering goods issue via 683 movement type

 

Hence based on item category determination NCCR delivery item is configured to be determined.

 

Once the item category is determined relevant Schedule line category is determined 

 

Based on the movement type maintained in the schedule line category, the goods issue will be triggered.

 

Setup condition record for SPED:

The system creates inbound deliveries with this item category when processing output type SPED during goods issued for an outbound delivery.

In transaction VV22 (Change Output - Condition Records: Shipping), ensure that in output determination for output type SPED, the condition records for the outbound delivery type used (NCCR, NCC3, NLIR, or NLI3) have been created as follows:

Output type SPED with role WE (Goods Recipient) or SH (Ship to Party), medium 8 (Special Function), and time 4 (Send Immediately (After Saving Application))

 

 

Process Flow Diagram:

 

 

Testing:

1. A Purchase Order is created to move stock between two EWM-managed warehouses

 

 

2. Outbound delivery is generated via VL10B

 

 

 

 

3. Outbound delivery is distributed to EWM Automatically

 

4. Warehouse Task Created

 

 

5. Warehouse Tasks are confirmed to complete the picking process

 

 

6. Goods issue posted in EWM

 

 

7. Goods issue is reflected back in outbound delivery in S/4 landscape by creating Material document with movement type 683 and 107.

 

8. HU information updated at the S/4 level

 

9. SPED output type is created and processed to create inbound delivery for receiving of stock.

 

10. Inbound delivery is created automatically and reflected in Purchase order

 

11. HU which were generated during repacking are attached to inbound delivery

12. Inbound Delivery gets distributed in the EWM system automatically.

 

13. Goods receipt and putaway is done in EWM system via /SCWM/PRDI or RF Device

14. Goods receipt is reflected in S/4 Landscape by creating a Material document with movement type 109

 

This completes the whole end to end process of intercompany STO between 2 EWM ,managed warehouses.

 

Regards,
Kamlesh Rampal.

entry.title: Re: Transport from ECC to S/4 Hana
entry.content: Yes, we have couple of reports and forms which need to move from ECC to S4 System.

entry.title: Re: I need to add a date dimension prompt to a universe
entry.content: I do not want to add the prompt as filter in the WHERE clause. I need it to be in the SELECT.

entry.title: Automating Rate Changes in SAP Fieldglass
entry.content:
Have you ever wondered if and how you can automate changes to a worker’s pay rate or to a supplier’s bill rate over the course of an assignment? 

Are there other better options than creating manual Work Order revisions? 

There are lots of reasons why rate changes may be needed but some of the most typical use cases include: 

  • Tenure discounts where a supplier’s margin is reduced after 1 year, for example 
  • Pay parity regulations that dictate increases to a worker’s pay rate over time, e.g., collective bargaining agreements (CBAs) such as ‘Branchenzuschläge’ (can you say the word?) in Germany 

Both of these examples require rate changes that follow defined logic and that also should be taken into account when calculating spend – especially when you are creating Purchase Requisitons and Purchase Orders on the back of your Fieldglass Work Orders. 

Wouldn’t it be great if these rate changes, and the logic that defines them, could be built into your SAP Fieldglass solution so that they are applied automatically, 

  1. at the right time 
  2. at the correct rate 
  3. defined for the entire duration of your Work Order and included into the Committed Spend calculation so that you can create one, and only one, correct Purchase Order in your ERP? 

What if you could define these rate changes from the buyer side to enforce tenure discounts you otherwise may not get from your suppliers? 

What if you could have peace of mind that collective bargaining agreements, and the worker pay rate increases they dictate, are applied correctly, automatically every time? 

Well guess what, you can! SAP Fieldglass has a rate automation feature that can be used on your contingent Work Orders – well actually, it has two! Buyer-defined Rate Change Matrix and Supplier-defined Rate Change Matrix can be leveraged to do just that. 

Both versions of Rate Change Matrix can be enabled on your Job Posting Templates under SAP Fieldglass’s traditional rate model – also referred to “Rates 1.0”. 

 Supplier-defined Rate Change Matrix is the simpler version of the two. Here the supplier needs to enter the rate change(s) when submitting a Job Seeker.  

You, as the buyer, can determine on your Job Posting Template if the supplier can calculate the target (pay or bill) rate manually by entering a distinct value that the rate should change to, or by entering a percentage increase or decrease instead. 

Same goes for the timing or frequency of the rate change – do you want your suppliers to manually enter a date for the change or do you want them to enter a “schedule” after which the change or changes occur? 

Rate Change Type and Rate Change Frequency settings on your Job Posting Templates can be used for that. 

Suppliers can also create Rate Change Matrix templates in their SAP Fieldglass supplier company admin menu to define these rate changes so that they don’t need to be manually entered every time they submit a  Job Seeker. 

What these supplier-defined options have in common is that they: 

  • Can be implemented with little configuration effort on the buyer side 
  • Do not define and enforce rate changes from the buyer side 

In other words, the benefit of the Supplier-defined Rate Change Matrix  from a buyer perspective is that it is fairly low-touch but you are still reliant on your suppliers doing the right thing by submitting correct information for your tenure discount or CBA dictated pay rate increases. 

And then there is the “buyer-defined” option of your Rate Change Type. This one is the deluxe version of the available rate change automation options in SAP Fieldglass. 

 

 Buyer-defined Rate Change Matrix can be used to automate and enforce rate changes, making sure that tenure discounts are correctly applied, or worker’s pay rates comply with legal and CBA regulations at all times. 

With a Buyer-defined Rate Change Matrix, rate change logic can leverage:   

  • Custom fields where you define, for example, an equal pay pay rate for your internal employees performing the same job as a temporary worker 
  • Custom lookups where you store publicly available pay rates per pay grade level from a CBA, together with their applicable validity dates or supplier markups you have individually agreed with your suppliers 
  • Custom business rules to calculate worker pay rates and/or supplier bill rates and how/when they change throughout the course of an assignment 

The buyer-defined Rate Change Matrix is more complex to set up and it requires the involvement of SAP Fieldglass technical resources. However, it is more robust in the sense that rate change logic is defined and enforced from the buyer side, and you do not need to depend on your suppliers to submit correct rate change information. 

For more information, check out the SAP Fieldglass Help Portal, contact your Customer Success Partner / Program Success Manager, or submit a case through SAP for Me or the SAP Fieldglass Help Center.

entry.title: Re: Story usage by user
entry.content:
Hello @alexander_john_berlin ,

Tracking Users Who Didn’t Open a Story in SAC you can do this using below methods

1.Audit Logs(In Security Menu)

Filter audit logs by story and time frame.
Compare with the total user list to find non-viewers.

2.Creating Live Data Model based on User's and Teams

Based on that you can create stories By User ID and Count of Session

Kindly like and Upvote if you find it useful!

entry.title: Flavors in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hallo SAP Support,

 

I want to know how to assign the flavors that I created in S/4HANA Cloud to the users and how to transport it?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Flavors in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

The KBA 3441818 - How to transport Adapt UI Personas Flavor in S/4HANA Cloud provides the information.

Follow the topics in Managing UI Adaptations for Classic Applications, so that flavors are available for users based on these assignment categories configuration:

  1. Understanding Assignment Categories
  2. Adapting UIs for Classic Applications
  3. Using Software Collections to Transport UI Adaptations
  4. Using Restrictions to Assign UI Adaptations to Business Roles

 

You can also check the Blog Post https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/getting-started-with-ui-adaptations-for-classic-applications-in-sap-s-4hana/ba-p/13545338 which is very detailed. 

 

Kind regards.

entry.title: Re: Help regarding App F6488 (Post Asset Acquisition – And Quantity Adjustment)
entry.content: Since it has been sometime since the question was opened and answered, we helped mark the answer as correct. Kind regards, Mark

entry.title: Re: UKM_BP error R1 206 when BP Role is not the standard...
entry.content: In fact, I was very disappointed with this being hardcoded. It makes no sense. Customization allows to create new BP Role and assign it to the UKM category and make it the standard role for this category. This is what SAP code should check. Not having UKM000 hardcoded. I created a case with SAP and their answer was "You have to use UKM000". Answer makes no sense for me. So, basically, we do not use UKM_BP.

entry.title: Re: Change Printer in RF EWM Public Cloud
entry.content: Dear @mconstanzo, We helped you to accept the answer, since there were no further updates under the topic. You can unaccept it anytime if the answer provided was not helpful enough and you have further questions. Best regards, Geovana

entry.title: Re: LSMW to create Mass User using BAPI Method
entry.content:
Hi Saikrishna,

I think the link is not available anymore. Do you have any other documentation for the same.

 

Thanks,

Akhil.

entry.title: Re: Updating the ShortText of an Inspection Lot
entry.content: Looking at the comments above, I suggest you use QA03 to view the long text you are interested in then use the menu to show the "Header" of thi long text. This will settle the question from janbuchman of which keys you should be using.

entry.title: Re: Functional Usage - Value Drivers and Peer Group Comparison
entry.content:
Hello,
while improvements to reporting  are nice, will there be any time and effort invested into fixing functionality gaps that still exist between the old SAP Support Portal and SAP for Me? 

e.g. the notifications allowing a removal by filtering for type of notification? I reported this back to at least 4 (!) different SAP contacts like over the past 2 years, but up to now nothing happened. Trying to put in improvements like this  via idea.sap.com (e.g. easy case creation with prefilled SW component for issues on SAP for Me applications) did also not help and were turned down since I did not spend my time to promote them on the SAP Community, therefore they did not get enough votes.
By the way: That functionality was there before  in the SAP Support Portal.
Regs
Ulf

entry.title: Table Bugging dimensions
entry.content:
Hello team,

I have a planning model with two dimensions: "Resources" and "Departments."
There are 5 departments, each with specific resources, as shown in the first image.

The issue is that when I add a new table, it creates duplicate "Resources" without any values in others departments. This disrupts one of our functionalities, preventing us from continuing with the project. I don’t understand why new "Resources" are being generated every time a new table is added.

I’ll provide an example of the "Resources" being created by the table, but this is just one case – the table often creates new resources for other departments randomly, with null values. Meanwhile, the original "Resource" with values remains in the correct department.
Inside the model, the resources are being created multiple times in other departments too, and the original still remains in the original department

Thanks for all the help, have a nice day

Bernardo Santos

entry.title: Re: AI in 2025 - predictions or guessing game
entry.content:
Hi,

It's true, DeepSeek has really stirred things up. It came out after I had already written my article, but I have to admit, it's quite impressive. Especially the low training cost. We'll see what consequences this will have and whether anyone will actually manage to replicate it in this way. This could potentially mean the democratization of model training.

Regarding data sovereignty awareness, it can be quite challenging these days. Indeed, such a discussion is likely to arise. However, countries that respect copyright laws might find themselves at a disadvantage compared to those that do not. But indeed, a lot has already happened in the commercial market when it comes to restricting access to data. Large providers are very often limiting access to their APIs...

entry.title: Re: Asset Accounting - Deprecation Below Zero
entry.content:
Hi,

The feature of "Deprecation Below Zero" is currently not supported in S4H Cloud for various reasons. In addition, the approach to use this method for cost accounting methods is a rather outdated approach. Also it demands a dedicated depreciation area which only posts depreciation to G/L. However, this feature does exist neither.

Best practice would be to use corporate valuation e.g. IFRS as approach for cost planning.

BR, 
Mick

entry.title: Re: Mass Change of Sales Documents
entry.content:
Hello Marce,

Is there any way to schedule this app in background. For example I want this app to run everyday and do the necessary action configured as a variant.

Thanks 

Nupur

entry.title: Using base, interface and consumption view for a entity
entry.content:
Dear community,

I was wondering about the sense of base views in an RAP context: Most of the documentation and tutorials I read claim that in RAP a single entity should be composed out of three CDS view layers: (1) base view, (2) interface view and (3) consumption view. This is also reflected in the showcase app.

However, from my experiences so far with RAP I find all three layers rather cumbersome. If one layer is skipped I think the base layer is the obvious choice. Entities consisting out of interface view and consumption view only can be seen for example in the DMO flight scenario.

I was wondering what are the experiences of others. Do you use all three layers or only two? What are your experiences there?

Kind regards

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content:
@graziela and @jbetanpr 

FYI:

BSI replied to my inquiry about WA PFML rates for 2025.  They said that it is targeted to be released in TUBS 158 scheduled for 01/08/2025.

entry.title: Re: Code List based on CDS View doesn't show View Entitie...
entry.content: The answer to my original question was given by @georg_wilhelm inside a comment of one of the other answers, his answer was: "currently one can only use Custom Entities as value help views for Key User Custom Fields. You can not use customer created view entities...even if you release them C1 for key users. We are aware of this gap and are already working on that."

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud transport traceability with SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hello Moritz,

I have setup integration scenario to connect SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Cloud ALM (SAP_COM_0690) on our public cloud development dev tenant. I can see and assign the TRs in the feature app after I release it BUT after assignment there is no target tenant filled in and when I execute a transport check I see below error. 

Can you advise on how to fix the issue?

Best wishes for 2025.

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content: @EPantino Thank you for sharing this!

entry.title: Is BusinessObjects Still Relevant in 2025?
entry.content:
In a world full of alternatives, is the SAP BusinessObjects platform still relevant to the enterprise? The answer is definitely “yes”, if for no other reason that SAP has invested in at least two more releases for the platform. Let’s quickly review what SAP BusinessObjects has going for it. In June 2022, the winds of change blew into the sails of the SAP BusinessObjects platform and BI 2025 was announced. In fact, it’s supposed to be delivered to us in a few short weeks. And last year, SAP committed to BI 2027, and BI 2029 is to be confirmed in H1.

Robust Server Architecture

What you call “vintage” I might call “proven”. The team that created the distributed architecture and Crystal Management Console admin tool for Crystal Enterprise many years ago should be pleased that this foundation and the rechristened Central Management Console are still largely at the heart of the BusinessObjects platform.

Operating System support

While SAP BusinessObjects supports fewer operating systems today than it has in the past, it’s a reflection of where the market has gone. The two operating systems that matter in 2025, Microsoft Windows Server and Linux, are both supported.

Cloud or On-premise?

Yes and yes. “Cloud” for many organizations now means their cloud in AWS, Azure, or GCP. Cloud vendors fully supported by SAP. And BusinessObjects can be hosted in SAP’s private cloud, for those customers who wish it.

Modern database connectivity

Take a look at the BI 4.3 platform availability matrix (PAM) and you’ll see the latest cloud-based databases like Snowflake, Databricks, AWS Redshift, Starburst, and Trino.

Powerful scheduling and bursting

Scheduling and bursting aren’t new features. But they can be lacking in some of the more “modern” analytics tools in the marketplace.

Robust semantic layer

The almighty BusinessObjects universe. The semantic layer is where many so-called modern tools fall short, with customers frequently resorting to using Microsoft Excel as database middleware.

Enterprise-Class Administration

If your organization is evaluating other tools, you’ll want to compare its administrator features against the Central Management Console. Make sure that something that performed adequately for a small number of POC users is ready to scale to the enterprise.

Modern User Experience

While desktop software was ironically central to a “land and expand” strategy for at least one of SAP’s analytic competitors, SAP provided a migration path from Desktop Intelligence many years ago. Meanwhile, having landed and expanded, those same competitors are furiously working to bring full-parity to their web-based design tools. And boasting “new” features such as tables because- news flash- enterprise reporting is still a core requirement after all.

And while browser plug-ins like Java and Adobe Flash served well in their time, they are nowhere to be found in the current BI 4.3 or the modern browsers SAP supports. SAP has provided us with a modern SAP Fiori inspired user experience.

New Features? Yeah, We’ve Got ‘Em

See Data Mode in SAP BI 4.3 SP4. Outdated systems do not add data cleansing features (or any features for that matter) to their core product.

Beware of FUD

FUD, or fear, uncertainty, and doubt, is a common marketing strategy. But be wary of the sales pitches. And demand proof and case studies to support vague claims.

BusinessObjects is an “Outdated System”

False. With frequent product updates and patches, SAP continues to modernize BusinessObjects for new browsers, operating systems, databases, and cloud computing. Let’s be clear here, “outdated systems” are operated by organizations who terminated SAP support and are nursing along legacy BI 4.1 or BI 4.2 without the benefit of patches for security issues or even simple matters like browser compatibility.

But We Have a Modern Cloud Architecture…

Ironically, with the advent of cloud computing, any software provider’s architecture is a black box. It doesn’t matter whether the architecture is a containerized warp drive or a data center filled with hundreds of clustered Raspberry PI – the value provided outside the black box is what truly matters.

Rising Costs?

The claim that staying on “outdated software” leads to rising costs demands proof. Migrating "outdated" analytics content to other tools only for the satisfaction of unplugging BusinessObjects is not bringing value to the business and brings its own budget busting challenges. Do you know what really raises costs? Software vendors using their “poster boy” advantage to muscle through price increases. Do the math, as small prudent investments in your BusinessObjects platform can bring much-needed innovation to your organization without the much larger cost of acquiring and rolling out new software.

What Next?

Technology doesn’t exist for technology’s sake. It exists to improve the success of the organization. Nothing lasts forever, but organizations that depend on BusinessObjects can continue do so for the foreseeable future with confidence.

What are your thoughts about the relevance of BusinessObjects in 2025? Share your comments below.

entry.title: Re: Extracting Documents via APIs in Onboarding 2.0
entry.content: Did you get any solution for above question.
